questions: Within the context of the seven stages of film production—where development centers on creating the screenplay and producing spans financing, marketing, and distribution—identify (1) the NYFA division dedicated to training writers who create the foundational scripts of visual storytelling, and (2) the fully online master’s degree designed to train entrepreneurial producers to operate at an executive level. For each, describe their key academic offerings, structure, and distinctive features. Include details such as degree types or program formats, locations or delivery mode, notable curriculum components, major projects or outcomes, program duration, and any available tuition information.

Within the stage of film production focused on development—where writers transform concepts into structured scripts—identify all of the fully online screenwriting workshops offered by the film academy that provides both in-person and remote programs in New York and Los Angeles. For each online workshop, specify: (1) its exact title, (2) its duration (e.g., 15-week or 4-week), (3) whether it requires a prerequisite or submission materials for admission, and (4) the primary script deliverable or learning outcome students complete by the end of the course. Your response must cover every distinct online screenwriting workshop currently listed by the institution.

rounds.answer_b:
TheNew York Film Academy (NYFA) **Screenwriting School** is the division dedicated to training writers who create the foundational scripts of visual storytelling. It offers AFA, BFA, and MFA degree programs at campuses in New York, Los Angeles, and South Beach. The curriculum focuses on core storytelling elements, utilizing intensive workshops in character development, dialogue, and scene structure. A distinctive feature is the "Table Reads," where student scripts are performed to provide feedback on narrative flow. Students graduate with a portfolio of original work, including feature screenplays and TV pilots. Program duration ranges from one year for conservatory programs to two or four years for degrees, with tuition varying by campus [1].

To train entrepreneurial producers for executive-level operations, NYFA offers the fully online **Master of Fine Arts (MFA) in Media Producing**. This two-year terminal degree is designed for students seeking leadership roles, covering the full spectrum of the producing cycle: financing, marketing, distribution, and physical production. The program includes a curriculum on media literacy, entertainment law, and film finance. The major outcome is a professional thesis project, which can be a feature film package or a new media proposal. A one-year **Master of Arts (MA)** in Film and Media Producing is also offered online. These programs provide flexible scheduling for working professionals, with tuition costs typically calculated per semester credit hour [2].

## References
[1] New York Film Academy, "Screenwriting School," NYFA.edu, https://www.nyfa.edu/screenwriting-school/
[2] New York Film Academy, "Online Media Producing School," NYFA.edu, https://www.nyfa.edu/online/producing-school/

rounds.answer_b:
New York Film Academy (NYFA), which provides in-person training in New York and Los Angeles, offers several distinct fully online screenwriting workshops specifically designed to support the development stage of filmmaking for remote learners.

The **Online Screenwriting Workshop** is a 15-week program designed for aspiring writers. It requires no prerequisite script samples for admission. The curriculum integrates live lectures and writing exercises to teach foundational elements like structure and dialogue. The primary deliverable is a completed first draft of a feature-length screenplay [1].

The **Online TV Writing Workshop** is a 15-week specialized course. Admission requires the submission of a writing sample to demonstrate storytelling potential. The curriculum covers episodic structure and industry standards for television. The key outcome is an original television pilot script, developed alongside a pitch bible and character breakdowns [1].

The **Online Screenplay Revision Workshop** is a 15-week advanced option. It requires the prerequisite submission of a completed feature screenplay. The curriculum emphasizes the rewriting process and critical script analysis. The main deliverable is a polished, professional-grade revision of the student's existing script, ready for market [1].

Additionally, NYFA provides **Online Short-Term Screenwriting Workshops** in 4, 6, 8, and 12-week formats. These workshops require no submission materials. They offer accelerated training in visual storytelling and scene construction. The deliverables include a story treatment and a completed short script, scaled appropriately to the duration of the course [2].

## References
[1] New York Film Academy, "Online Screenwriting School," NYFA.edu, https://www.nyfa.edu/online/screenwriting-school/
[2] New York Film Academy, "Online Short-Term Screenwriting Workshops," NYFA.edu, https://www.nyfa.edu/online/short-term-workshops/
